mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. A 3-month rolling decline in FEDFUNDS of at least 25 basis points activates the signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines by at least 0.25pp over preceding 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 2.28% suggests room for easing from recent tightening cycle
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines of 0.25pp+ are not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, supporting equity valuations through higher expected corporate earnings. An increase of 100k+ jobs month-over-month activates positive forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by at least 100,000 persons in the most recent month
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 132,573k indicates sustained employment levels
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with robust labor market
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of months where PAYEMS rises 100k+, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) lead consumer spending and economic activity. When HOUST falls below 1,800k annualized units, it signals weakening demand that predicts negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk rises.
evidence_refs: fact:Current HOUST at 2,004k is above threshold but near cyclical sensitivity zone
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with demand deceleration risk
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of periods where HOUST < 1,800k, or the correlation between HOUST changes and forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) reflects real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while UNRATE rises simultaneously, it signals stagflation risk that produces negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth < 0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in preceding month
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 117.813 and UNRATE at 5.2% require joint monitoring for stagflation signals
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with inflation and supply constraints
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the joint signal has Rank IC < 0.02 when tested independently of single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly indicate consumer demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth > 1%, it signals robust demand that produces positive 20-day equity returns through earnings growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both increase > 1% month-over-month in the same month
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 347,665M and HOUST at 2,004k suggest demand resilience
regime_hint: mid-to-late cycle expansion with synchronized demand growth
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) when controlling for single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS below 3% signals monetary policy lag that eventually forces tightening, producing negative 60-day equity returns as rate hike expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:Current CPIAUCSL at 191.1 and FEDFUNDS at 2.28% create policy lag scenario
regime_hint: monetary policy lag in inflationary environment
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the joint signal fails to predict rate hike cycles with > 60% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 5.5% signals labor market deterioration that predicts negative equity returns over 60 trading days as recession risk increases and earnings expectations decline.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 5.2% is near but below threshold, indicating labor market tightness
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 52% of months where UNRATE > 5.5%, or the predictive power deteriorates (Rank IC < 0.02) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) sustained growth above 1% month-over-month signals expanding real economic capacity and productivity, producing positive 5-day equity returns through improved profit margins and growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 117.813 reflects ongoing industrial activity
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply-side momentum
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in fewer than 53% of months with INDPRO growth > 1%, or the correlation between INDPRO growth and 5-day forward returns is not significantly positive (p > 0.05).
